product.name: GitHub non-code product platform
product.goal: Reproduce GitHub product semantics for people, enterprises, organizations, teams, repositories, collaboration, engagement, governance, commerce, and integrations without implementing source-code or Git capabilities.

excludedCapabilities.name: git-and-repository-content
excludedCapabilities.reason: Git objects, files, commits, refs, branches, tags, clone, fetch, push, and repository content are outside the product boundary.

excludedCapabilities.name: diff-merge-and-pull-requests
excludedCapabilities.reason: Diffs, mergeability, merge execution, pull requests, reviews, and code-review state require the excluded code domain.

excludedCapabilities.name: actions-and-code-products
excludedCapabilities.reason: Actions execution, code search and navigation, code security analysis, Dependabot, Packages, Pages, and Codespaces are excluded.

deferredCapabilities.name: forks-and-templates
deferredCapabilities.requires: Git history and repository-content provisioning providers.

deferredCapabilities.name: community-profile
deferredCapabilities.requires: A repository-content provider for community files.

deferredCapabilities.name: code-rulesets
deferredCapabilities.requires: Branch, tag, push, and code-governance resources.

deferredCapabilities.name: repository-traffic-metrics
deferredCapabilities.requires: A trustworthy product-telemetry ingestion, retention, and aggregation capability.

deferredCapabilities.name: repository-wiki-content
deferredCapabilities.requires: A trustworthy Git-backed wiki content provider.

deferredCapabilities.name: repository-migration-locks
deferredCapabilities.requires: A repository migration orchestration capability with documented lock ownership and recovery.

deferredCapabilities.name: organization-discussion-source-repository-disruption
deferredCapabilities.requires: Documented GitHub behavior or an accepted product policy for source repository transfer and deletion.

contexts.name: accounts
contexts.responsibility: User account identity, personal or managed account type, human or machine usage, username, lifecycle, and ghost attribution.

semanticClaims.statement: A user account has a username; it may be personal or enterprise-managed, while machine user describes account usage rather than a third account type.
Deleting a personal account changes resource attribution to the ghost user and eventually permits username reuse.

contexts.name: authentication
contexts.responsibility: Credentials, browser session sets, active account-session selection, two-factor authentication, recovery, and external login binding.

semanticClaims.statement: A browser can retain multiple account sessions, selects one active session, and requires reauthentication before an expired managed-user session can be activated.
Authentication owns two-factor configuration and external login binding lifecycle.

contexts.name: profiles
contexts.responsibility: Public and private personal profiles, profile status, and pinned-item presentation.

contexts.name: social-graph
contexts.responsibility: Following relationships between users and organizations.

contexts.name: enterprises
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise identity, profile, account mode, lifecycle, and authoritative organization ownership links.

supports: enterprise accounts do not directly own repositories

semanticClaims.statement: An enterprise account has identity and lifecycle semantics and contains organizations; it does not directly own repositories.

contexts.name: enterprise-memberships
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise membership, invitations, affiliation, guest collaborators, and unaffiliated users.

semanticClaims.statement: Enterprise people views distinguish active members, unaffiliated users, and guest collaborators without embedding administrative roles in membership.

contexts.name: enterprise-teams
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise-wide teams used for centralized role, organization, and license assignment.

contexts.name: enterprise-roles
contexts.responsibility: Predefined and custom enterprise roles, permissions, and assignments.

semanticClaims.statement: Enterprise administrative abilities come from predefined or custom role assignments and permissions, not from membership alone.

contexts.name: enterprise-iam
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise identity-provider configuration, SAML or OIDC authentication, SCIM provisioning, and group synchronization.

contexts.name: enterprise-policies
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise policy constraints applied across owned organizations and repositories.

contexts.name: custom-properties
contexts.responsibility: Enterprise-defined repository and organization custom-property schemas, organization values, and promotion of organization repository properties.

publishedEvents.name: EnterpriseRepositoryPropertyPromoted
publishedEvents.meaning: organization repository property promoted to enterprise authority.

semanticClaims.statement: Enterprises define repository properties, defaults, edit policies, and promotion behavior.
Enterprises define organization properties and organizations hold their assigned values.

contexts.name: organizations
contexts.responsibility: Organization identity, login, profile, lifecycle, and verified domains.

semanticClaims.statement: An organization has its own identity, profile, login, lifecycle, and verified-domain semantics, but it cannot authenticate as a user account.

contexts.name: organization-memberships
contexts.responsibility: Organization membership, invitations, member roles, and membership lifecycle.

semanticClaims.statement: Organization membership has member or owner roles and lifecycle states; pending, suspended, and removed memberships are not active membership.

contexts.name: organization-teams
contexts.responsibility: Organization teams, nested hierarchy, visibility, membership, maintainers, and mentions.

semanticClaims.statement: Organization teams contain active organization members, and a team maintainer must remain a direct member of that team.
Nested organization teams form an acyclic same-organization hierarchy, while secret teams do not participate in parent-child nesting.

contexts.name: organization-roles
contexts.responsibility: Predefined and custom organization roles and custom repository-role definitions.

semanticClaims.statement: Organization owners can assign multiple predefined organization roles to existing organization members or organization teams.
Organizations can define custom organization roles and custom repository roles in addition to immutable predefined organization roles.

contexts.name: organization-policies
contexts.responsibility: Organization policies for repositories, collaborators, projects, discussions, and member privileges.

contexts.name: custom-properties
contexts.responsibility: Organization-defined repository custom-property schemas and repository property values from organization or enterprise definitions.

publishedEvents.name: OrganizationRepositoryPropertyPromotionRequested
publishedEvents.meaning: organization repository property submitted for enterprise promotion.

semanticClaims.statement: Organizations define repository property schemas, allowed values, defaults, and explicit requirements.
Repositories hold property values and the source of each assigned value.

contexts.name: repositories
contexts.responsibility: Repository identity, personal or organization ownership, name, description, homepage, visibility, lifecycle, redirects, and transfer.

supports: public repositories for a specified user

publishedEvents.name: RepositoryTransferRequested
publishedEvents.meaning: repository transfer to another personal account requested and awaiting acceptance.

publishedEvents.name: RepositoryTransferExpired
publishedEvents.meaning: repository transfer invitation to another personal account expired after one day without acceptance.

semanticClaims.statement: A repository has GitHub identity, ownership, description, homepage, and visibility semantics.
Renaming a repository creates repository redirect behavior.
Repository visibility changes have explicit product effects.
Repository transfer always records completion, while request and one-day expiry outcomes apply only when another personal account must accept the transfer.
Repository lifecycle distinguishes active, archived, and deleted behavior without claiming migration-lock ownership.

contexts.name: repository-access
contexts.responsibility: Repository invitations, direct and inherited grants, outside collaborators, role assignments, and source-attributed effective permission resolution.

publishedEvents.name: RepositoryInvitationPermissionChanged
publishedEvents.meaning: permissions on an open repository invitation changed before acceptance.

publishedEvents.name: RepositoryInvitationDeclined
publishedEvents.meaning: repository invitation declined by the invitee.

semanticClaims.statement: Open repository collaboration invitations can have their future repository permission changed before they are accepted, declined, or revoked.
Repository grants and role assignments contribute to source-attributed effective permissions.
Organization repositories support direct and inherited team access with lifecycle effects.
Organization repository access can be granted to and revoked from outside collaborators.

contexts.name: repository-features
contexts.responsibility: Repository Issues, Discussions, Projects, and Wiki enablement with feature-specific configuration.

publishedEvents.name: RepositoryIssuesFeatureChanged
publishedEvents.meaning: repository Issues enablement or creation policy changed.

publishedEvents.name: RepositoryWikiDisabled
publishedEvents.meaning: repository Wiki disabled without erasing its content.

semanticClaims.statement: Repository Issues can be disabled while preserving existing issues and can restrict creation to collaborators.
Repository Discussions has repository-scoped enablement controlled by repository administrators.
The repository Projects tab can be configured without deleting linked projects.
Repository Wikis can be disabled and re-enabled without erasing existing wiki content.

contexts.name: repository-metadata
contexts.responsibility: Repository topics and social-media preview configuration.

semanticClaims.statement: A repository can have up to 20 topics; each topic is at most 50 characters, uses lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, and its name is public even for a private repository.
Repository social-preview images can be changed or removed subject to GitHub's image format, size, and repository-visibility restrictions.

contexts.name: issues
contexts.responsibility: Issue lifecycle, assignment, hierarchy, dependency, transfer, and work tracking.

contexts.name: issue-schema
contexts.responsibility: Organization-level issue type and field definitions, visibility, pinning, and type-field associations.

contexts.name: labels-and-milestones
contexts.responsibility: Repository-scoped labels, milestones, and work classification.

contexts.name: conversations
contexts.responsibility: Capability-constrained comments, discussion replies, reactions, mentions, revisions, and locks for a closed set of subjects.

contexts.name: discussions
contexts.responsibility: Repository discussion forums and organization discussion spaces, source-repository binding, categories, sections, polls, answers, pins, and lifecycle.

supports: poll voting without a poll-close mutation

publishedEvents.name: DiscussionCategoryDeleted
publishedEvents.meaning: discussion category deleted after its discussions were moved to a selected existing category.

publishedEvents.name: DiscussionSectionDeleted
publishedEvents.meaning: discussion section deleted while its categories remained unsectioned.

publishedEvents.name: OrganizationDiscussionSpaceEnabled
publishedEvents.meaning: organization discussion space enabled with a source repository.

publishedEvents.name: OrganizationDiscussionSourceChanged
publishedEvents.meaning: organization discussion source repository changed without transferring existing discussions.

semanticClaims.statement: Repositories and organizations expose discussion forums with up to 25 uniquely named-and-emoji-paired categories; each category can belong to at most one section, category deletion moves discussions to another category, and section deletion leaves categories unsectioned.
Discussions can be closed, reopened, deleted with their replies, or transferred subject to GitHub's repository and announcement restrictions.

Answerable discussions support marking eligible non-minimized comments as answers, discussions can be pinned, and poll discussions require at least two options without claiming a separate poll-close operation.
Organization Discussions requires an organization-owned source repository and derives permissions from it.
Changing the Organization Discussions source repository does not transfer existing discussions.

contexts.name: moderation
contexts.responsibility: Content reports, moderation cases, blocks, interaction limits, and visibility decisions.

contexts.name: projects
contexts.responsibility: User- or organization-owned projects, items, draft issues, views, fields, workflows, charts, templates, and status updates.

contexts.name: stars
contexts.responsibility: Repository starring and user-defined star lists for discovery and collection.

contexts.name: subscriptions
contexts.responsibility: Repository watch preferences, conversation participation and manual subscriptions, ignore preferences, and notification-interest decisions.

contexts.name: notifications
contexts.responsibility: User notification records, inboxes, reasons, filters, and read, saved, or done state.

publishedEvents.name: NotificationDeliveryRequested
publishedEvents.meaning: notification delivery requested after recipient and read-access resolution.

contexts.name: github-app-registrations
contexts.responsibility: GitHub App registration, ownership and ownership transfer, requested permissions, webhook preference, requested webhook events, and visibility.

publishedEvents.name: GitHubAppDeleted
publishedEvents.meaning: GitHub App registration deleted and its installations invalidated.

semanticClaims.statement: GitHub Apps have registrations, owners, permission requests, and visibility settings.
GitHub App registration updates include permissions and webhook configuration.
GitHub App registration ownership can be transferred to eligible accounts.
Deleting a GitHub App registration removes its installations.

contexts.name: github-app-installations
contexts.responsibility: GitHub App installation targets, selected repositories, granted permissions, suspension, and uninstall lifecycle.

publishedEvents.name: GitHubAppInstallationPermissionsChanged
publishedEvents.meaning: installation permission grant changed after the installation owner approved additional permissions.

semanticClaims.statement: GitHub App installations bind an app to a user or organization target with repository selection and an approved permission grant.
A registration can request additional permissions, but an installation permission grant changes only after its owner approves; without approval the installation retains its current permissions.
GitHub App installations can be suspended and unsuspended by authorized actors.
Installation repository selection can change and installations can be removed.

contexts.name: oauth-app-registrations
contexts.responsibility: OAuth App registration, ownership, callback configuration, and client lifecycle.

contexts.name: oauth-authorizations
contexts.responsibility: User authorization of registered OAuth Apps, scopes, approval, and revocation.

contexts.name: repository-autolinks
contexts.responsibility: Repository-scoped autolink definitions for external resource references.

semanticClaims.statement: An administrator can configure an entitled repository autolink with a non-overlapping prefix, numeric or alphanumeric identifier format, and target URL containing the <num> placeholder.
Repository autolinks can be deleted; no in-place update capability is claimed.

contexts.name: webhooks
contexts.responsibility: Repository, organization, and enterprise webhook configuration plus GitHub App webhook projections, deliveries, attempts, and redelivery.

contexts.name: billing
contexts.responsibility: Billing accounts, payment profiles, usage, budgets, cost centers, invoices, and spending allocation.

contexts.name: entitlements
contexts.responsibility: Plans, feature entitlements, licenses, assignments, and usage limits.

contexts.name: audit-logs
contexts.responsibility: Organization and enterprise audit events, scopes, actors, targets, search, export, streaming, and retention policy.

contexts.name: search
contexts.responsibility: Permission-filtered search projections across users, organizations, repositories, issues, discussions, and projects.
contexts.eventRationale: Read-model context consumes versioned events and does not publish product facts.

contexts.name: dashboard
contexts.responsibility: Available and selected personal or organization Dashboard contexts plus permission-filtered repository views.
contexts.eventRationale: The Dashboard is a read-model projection and does not publish product facts.

semanticClaims.statement: The Dashboard can switch between the authenticated user's personal context and organizations where the user has an active membership; enterprise administration is a separate surface.
A Dashboard repository view scopes by selected owner context and remains independently filtered by effective repository permissions.

contexts.name: activity-feed
contexts.responsibility: User-visible dashboard and resource activity projections.
contexts.eventRationale: Read-model context consumes versioned events and does not publish product facts.

contexts.name: repository-insights
contexts.responsibility: Non-code repository engagement trends and integration-health projections.
contexts.eventRationale: Read-model context consumes versioned events and does not publish product facts.

contexts.name: event-publication
contexts.responsibility: Dispatch, leasing, retry, operational idempotency, redelivery, and dead-letter handling for context-owned event envelopes.

contexts.name: search-index
contexts.responsibility: Search document indexing, querying, and index lifecycle adapters.

contexts.name: media-storage
contexts.responsibility: Storage and retrieval of media referenced by product domains.

contexts.name: notification-channels
contexts.responsibility: External email or push delivery adapters for accepted notification delivery requests.

contexts.name: audit-storage
contexts.responsibility: Storage, export, and retention enforcement for audit records.
